context.region: United States (multiple locations: Texas, Pennsylvania, California)
context.operator: Multiple operators (Gulf Oil, Formosa Plastics, Valero, ITC, KMCO, Philadelphia Energy Solutions, TPC Group)

event.incident_type: Multiple incidents involving lack of remote isolation capability
actions_taken: CSB issued recommendations to API, EPA, and OSHA
API updated RP 2001 to include Section 5.4.3.4.2 on ROSOVs
CCPS developed Remote Isolation of Process Equipment guidance
NTSB issued recommendations on pipeline remote isolation
Facilities installed remotely operated emergency isolation valves
event.summary: This CSB Safety Study examines six major incidents (1975-2019) where lack of effective remote isolation equipment contributed to escalation of loss-of-containment events, resulting in fatalities, serious injuries, environmental contamination, and massive property damage. The study concludes that regulatory requirements for remote isolation of process equipment are necessary to prevent future incidents.
recommendations: API: Develop new publication or revise existing publications requiring remote isolation devices for major process equipment and atmospheric storage tanks with mandatory 'shall' language
EPA: Update Risk Management Program rule to include evaluation of remote isolation devices for major process equipment
OSHA: Expand Process Safety Management standard to include evaluation of remote isolation devices in Process Hazard Analysis requirements
Industry: Assess remote isolation capabilities for major process equipment during hazard reviews and Process Hazard Analysis
Facilities: Install remotely operated emergency isolation valves configured to fail-closed for atmospheric storage tanks containing highly hazardous chemicals

hazards.name: Flammable liquid or gas inventory in process equipment
Toxic chemical inventory in process equipment
Corrosive chemical inventory in process equipment
Atmospheric storage tanks containing hazardous materials

threats.name: Popcorn polymer accumulation in dead leg piping
Fire or heat exposure to equipment

controls.name: Remotely Operated Emergency Isolation Valves (ROEIVs)
controls.lod_basis: Allows operators to stop release from safe location after loss of containment occurs

supporting_text: operators were unable to reach the manual valves capable of stopping the release due to an advancing vapor cloud that forced them to retreat
had a remotely operated valve been installed upstream of the pumps supplying the propylene, the propylene flow could have been halted
had remote control of the pumps been possible from the control room, the propylene flow could have been quickly reduced
Tank 80-8 and the other aboveground storage tanks located in the tank farm were not equipped with remotely operated emergency isolation valves, or ROEIVs

had KMCO workers been able to close the actuated block valve installed just upstream of the y-strainer from the control room, the amount of isobutylene released and the subsequent harm to workers could have been greatly reduced
there were no ROEIVs installed in the HF alkylation unit to remotely and automatically isolate the large hydrocarbon sources adjacent to the failed elbow

controls.name: Remotely Operated Shutoff Valves (ROSOVs)
controls.barrier_role: Rapid isolation of pressurized flammable material
controls.lod_basis: Allows control room operators to shut off flow from safe location during emergency

supporting_text: Control room operators were unable to shut off the flow of propane because there were no remotely operable shut-off valves, or ROSOVs, in the PDA unit
the lack of remote isolation significantly increased the size and duration of the fire
ROSOVs should be used in areas, such as the PDA unit, where fast and effective isolation is needed to reduce the impact of major hazardous releases

controls.name: Emergency Block Valves (EBVs) - Type D (Remotely Operated)
controls.barrier_role: Remote isolation of flammable or toxic substances
controls.lod_basis: Type D EBVs with remote controls located minimum 40 feet from leak source and out of fire zone

supporting_text: had the TPC facility been equipped with 'Type D' EBVs, the feed to the Final Fractionator A column could have been stopped shortly after the release began

Manual and locally controlled emergency block valves ('Type A,' 'Type B,' and 'Type C,' as defined by API RP 553) serve no reliable function in catastrophic incidents, since the valves often cannot be safely accessed during these events
Equipment that handles large inventories of flammable or toxic material should be equipped with 'Type D' remotely operated emergency isolation valves

controls.name: Fail-Closed Valve Configuration
controls.barrier_role: Automatic isolation on power loss or fire event
controls.lod_basis: Ensures valve closes automatically if power is lost or fire occurs, preventing continued release

supporting_text: ITC did not equip these tanks with shut-off valves that would fail closed, for example by installing fusible link valves, programming logic, or other protective measures
The ROEIVs should be equipped with fusible links or configured to automatically close in the event of a power outage or other event ('Fail-Closed')

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) - Remote Isolation Evaluation
controls.barrier_role: Identification of need for remote isolation equipment
controls.lod_basis: Systematic review to determine when remote isolation devices are required for major process equipment

supporting_text: Formosa had addressed isolation of minor leaks, the company's written hazard analysis did not consider a catastrophic loss of containment within the unit
the McKee Refinery's process hazard analysis (PHA) failed to identify and address the need for ROSOVs in the PDA unit
Neither KMCO's 2014 PHA nor its 2015 PHA included any discussion of remote isolation or recommendations to equip the isobutylene system with remote isolation valves
the need for this type of remote isolation equipment would have been identified through hazard assessments, such as those required by the OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) standard

controls.name: CCPS Remote Isolation Decision Tool
controls.barrier_role: Systematic evaluation of remote isolation necessity
controls.lod_basis: Flow chart tool to determine whether remote isolation equipment should be installed based on material properties and operating conditions

supporting_text: the CCPS provides a flow chart as an example of a tool that companies can use to determine whether remote isolation equipment should be installed
The CSB found in its KMCO investigation that had this tool been applied to KMCO's isobutylene system, remote isolation equipment should have been provided
The CSB applied this tool, which has been available since 2003, to the other incidents discussed in this Safety Study, all of which occurred after 2003
The CSB determined that had the tool been applied to each facility's system that experienced the loss of containment, effective remote isolation equipment should have been provided

controls.name: Industry Standards and Guidance (API RP 2001, API RP 553, CCPS Guidelines)
controls.barrier_role: Establish requirements and best practices for remote isolation
controls.lod_basis: Voluntary industry standards providing guidance on remote isolation design and installation

supporting_text: although good industry guidance has been available for many years, the guidance has not been consistently followed
the CSB has concluded that reliance on existing industry guidance concerning remote isolation alone is not sufficient
While strengthening voluntary standards is important, the CSB has concluded that regulatory requirements associated with remote isolation of process equipment are necessary

API RP 2001 states, 'Where a review establishes a need, remotely operated shutoff valves (ROSOV) should be considered during the PHA and FHA processes.' The distinction between 'a review' that 'establishes a need' and 'the PHA and FHA processes' is unclear

controls.name: Regulatory Requirements (OSHA PSM, EPA RMP)
controls.barrier_role: Mandate evaluation of remote isolation in process safety management
controls.lod_basis: Federal regulations requiring Process Hazard Analysis and Risk Management Program evaluations

supporting_text: because of an exemption related to atmospheric storage tanks, the PSM standard did not apply to the tanks in the tank farm
The EPA RMP rule also did not apply due to ITC's characterization of the flammability of the butane-enriched naphtha product
CSB Recommendation No. 2019-01-I-TX-R7 to OSHA: Eliminate the atmospheric storage tank exemption from the PSM standard
CSB Recommendation No. 2019-01-I-TX-R8 to EPA: Modify 40 CFR §68.115(b)(2)(i) to expand coverage of the RMP rule to include all flammable liquids

controls.name: Insurance Audits and Risk Assessments
controls.barrier_role: Identification of remote isolation gaps through third-party review
controls.lod_basis: Insurance company audits to identify process safety deficiencies

supporting_text: An insurance audit conducted for the ITC Deer Park terminal in October 2018 indicated that storage tanks located in another area of the terminal were equipped with electric motor operated valves (MOVs)
The CSB noted that in April 2016, in connection with a review of the facility, the insurance company FM Global had observed that the butadiene process unit was not equipped with ROEIVs
FM Global recommended that TPC 'improve remote isolation capabilities within the tank farm and within the process units'

controls.name: Manual Isolation Valves (Type A, B, C EBVs)
controls.barrier_role: Manual isolation of hazardous material release
controls.lod_basis: Manually operated fire-safe block valves at equipment or nearby location

supporting_text: operators were unable to reach the manual valves capable of stopping the release due to an advancing vapor cloud that forced them to retreat
firefighters had to take extreme efforts to access and close the valve on the tank manually
firefighters had to push a rowboat through a tank dike that was flooded with hot crude oil, foam, and water as the fire burned around them

Manual and locally controlled emergency block valves ('Type A,' 'Type B,' and 'Type C,' as defined by API RP 553) serve no reliable function in catastrophic incidents, since the valves often cannot be safely accessed during these events

controls.name: Pump Shutdown Controls
controls.barrier_role: Stop flow of hazardous material by shutting down supply pump
controls.lod_basis: Remote or local control of pumps supplying hazardous material

supporting_text: operators were also unable to reach the local control station to quickly turn off the pumps supplying propylene
had remote control of the pumps been possible from the control room, the propylene flow could have been quickly reduced

controls.name: Dead Leg Elimination and Maintenance Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent accumulation of reactive materials in piping
controls.lod_basis: Proper maintenance procedures to eliminate dead legs that can accumulate hazardous materials

supporting_text: a dangerous substance known as popcorn polymer, which can form in processes with high-purity butadiene, accumulated in a temporary dead leg in piping at the facility that was created when a process pump was taken out of service for maintenance
During the offline period, popcorn polymer developed and exponentially expanded in the dead leg piping section until the pressure in the piping increased to the point that the piping ruptured

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
